standards.title: Data protection by design and default (Art 25)
Consumer rights to know, delete, correct

evidence.quote: A local, restart-safe HTTP API for organization-scoped notes.
no third-party runtime or development dependencies and makes no external
The local Tenant Notes API and its two fixed organizations.
Structured exclusions: UI has no rendered surface; deploy and launch have no
Use one SQLite file per organization. Authentication hashes the supplied bearer
Flip point: a third organization is required, store-open time exceeds 100 ms
There is no backup promise. For evaluator data, stop the process and restore

export function toProblem(error: unknown, instance: string): ProblemDetails {
test("known cross-organization ids are indistinguishable from absent ids", async () => {
test("organization members can complete CRUD across peer-owned notes", async () => {
Pillars 1.1 present but compatible=false: loader has no structured YAML exclusions; pillar list fields and body headings fail the 1.1 validator.

strengths.title: Tenant isolation uses independent physical and logical boundaries
strengths.preserve: Keep store selection authentication-bound, every note query organization-scoped, and the composite owner/organization foreign key intact.

strengths.title: The dependency-free supply chain is enforced and inventoried
strengths.preserve: Retain the locked zero-dependency policy or update the SBOM and supply-chain gate with any deliberate dependency.

strengths.title: API promises map to contract, handlers, and static test definitions
strengths.preserve: Keep README, OpenAPI, route behavior, and black-box test definitions synchronized.

strengths.title: Shared error and logging boundaries redact sensitive fields
strengths.preserve: Route new errors and log events through the central mapper and sanitizer.

findings.title: The artifact has no version-control or CI provenance
findings.impact: Reviewers cannot establish change history, tracked-artifact hygiene, branch protections, full-history secret scanning, or that the authoritative verification gate ran for this built artifact.
findings.fix: Place the artifact in a Git repository and add a least-privilege CI workflow that installs from the lockfile and runs npm run verify, including the Pillars, migration, dependency, secret, and architecture checks.

findings.title: Product decisions are scattered without a lightweight owning brief
findings.impact: A maintainer can reconstruct the evaluator and isolation promise, but not the appetite, cut rule, ranked acceptance criteria, full NFR disposition, or owned risks and assumptions.
findings.fix: Add docs/product-brief.md that reuses the README, context pillar, ADR, and existing tests while recording the missing product decisions.

findings.title: Product success is not computable from a metric-to-event contract
findings.impact: The API emits useful operational events, but no named leading or lagging outcome, deadline, source, or computation establishes whether the evaluator product succeeds.
findings.fix: Add docs/metrics.md with at most five outcomes mapped to existing structured events or deterministic verification results.

findings.title: Stack decision provenance is incomplete
findings.impact: The tenant-isolation ADR has concrete flip points and rejected alternatives, but lacks the dated preflight, planning horizon, support basis, and switching-cost record needed to revisit the runtime and database bundle safely.
findings.fix: Add a compact dated stack decision that incorporates the existing ADR and records domain/team/budget/time/regulatory basis, runner-up, 12-month ceiling, recheck condition, and engineer-week switching cost.
findings.verify: rg -n '^(Date|Domain|Team|Budget posture|Time-to-ship|Regulatory statement|Runner-up|Flip point|Scale ceiling|Switching cost|Re-check):' docs/stack/decision.md

findings.title: README runtime support contradicts the manifest
findings.impact: A user may select Node 25 or newer from the README while npm metadata explicitly rejects that runtime.
findings.fix: Choose the supported Node range and make package.json, package-lock.json, and README state the same contract.

findings.title: The claimed Pillars 1.1 memory is structurally incompatible
findings.impact: Future agents receive useful content, but portable routing and complete Core concern accounting are ambiguous because the loader lacks structured exclusions and pillar frontmatter is not valid Pillars 1.1.
findings.fix: Convert exclusions and selector fields to the 1.1 schema, preserve the two always-loaded floor pillars, and make the repository validator use the same grammar as the declared standard.

findings.title: POST note creation has no retry idempotency contract
findings.impact: A client retry after an ambiguous response can create duplicate notes because no idempotency key is accepted or durably deduplicated.
findings.fix: Accept an Idempotency-Key for POST /v1/notes and persist a tenant-scoped unique key with the created note in the same SQLite transaction.

findings.title: Ordinary authentication denials omit source identity from security logs
findings.impact: Operators can identify denial spikes, but cannot correlate individual denied requests to a source IP during an investigation.
findings.fix: Pass the normalized remote address into the centralized denial log while preserving token and note redaction.

findings.title: Hard-deleted notes have no recovery path
findings.impact: An accidental authenticated DELETE permanently removes evaluator notes, and the runbook explicitly provides no backup promise.
findings.fix: Either document hard delete as an intentional evaluator-only limit with a client confirmation requirement, or add a short retention trash state and restore operation.
findings.verify: rg -n 'trash|restore|delete confirmation|intentional hard delete' README.md docs src openapi.json

tasks.title: Establish Git and CI provenance for the authoritative gate
tasks.reuses: the existing npm run verify chain
acceptance: The artifact is committed in Git and git rev-parse succeeds.
CI installs the lockfile with scripts disabled and runs npm run verify on push and pull request.
Secret and Pillars checks fail the workflow rather than soft-failing.
tasks.verify: git rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree && rg -n 'npm ci.*ignore-scripts|npm run verify' .github/workflows/ci.yml

tasks.title: Complete the stack decision record
tasks.reuses: ADR 001 flip points and rejected alternatives
acceptance: The record reuses the accepted isolation ADR instead of duplicating it.
It records a date, six-part preflight, 12-month ceiling, runner-up, switching cost, and re-check condition.
tasks.verify: rg -n '^(Date|Domain|Team|Budget posture|Time-to-ship|Regulatory statement|Runner-up|Flip point|Scale ceiling|Switching cost|Re-check):' docs/stack/decision.md

tasks.title: Align the Node runtime support contract
tasks.reuses: package.json engines as the canonical machine contract
acceptance: One supported Node range is chosen deliberately.
Manifest, lock metadata, and README state exactly the same range.

tasks.title: Make agent memory conform to Pillars 1.1
tasks.reuses: the existing context, quality, and repo pillar content
acceptance: Loader exclusions use structured name/reason records and all Core concerns are accounted for.
Frontmatter list fields and eight body sections validate under Pillars 1.1.
Routing fixtures cover whole-word matching and the two floor pillars remain always loaded.

tasks.title: Write the lightweight product brief
tasks.reuses: README scope, context pillar, ADR 001, and isolation tests
acceptance: Brief records primary user, appetite, cut rule, ranked GWT requirements, NFR dispositions, risks, and assumptions.
Existing README, context, ADR, OpenAPI, and tests are linked instead of copied.

tasks.title: Define product metrics from existing events
tasks.reuses: note action logs, request_denied, auth_denial_spike, and deterministic verify results
acceptance: At most five numbered and deadlined outcomes include one leading and one lagging metric.
Every metric names an existing structured event or deterministic verification source and an exact computation.

tasks.title: Make note creation idempotent
tasks.reuses: the existing SQLite repository boundary and OpenAPI generator
acceptance: POST accepts Idempotency-Key and documents its response semantics.
A tenant-scoped unique key and note creation commit in one transaction.
A regression test proves a replay returns the original result without a second note.
The migration is idempotent and covered by the authoritative verification gate.

tasks.title: Include source IP in denied-request logs
acceptance: Every authentication denial log includes the normalized remote address.
Bearer tokens and note title/body values remain redacted.
A static regression assertion covers the ordinary denial path and spike path.

tasks.title: Make hard-delete recovery semantics explicit
tasks.reuses: the current DELETE route and recovery runbook
acceptance: The evaluator-only hard-delete decision and client confirmation expectation are explicit, or a trash/restore lifecycle is implemented.
OpenAPI and runbook agree on recovery behavior.
tasks.verify: rg -n 'trash|restore|delete confirmation|intentional hard delete' README.md docs openapi.json src/routes/contract.ts

tasks.title: Re-run godaudits at the remediated commit
tasks.reuses: the current check catalog and evidence fingerprint
acceptance: Every active remediation task is complete.
Fresh evidence validates and score plus coverage deltas are recorded.

session_log.summary: Fresh full static audit; no project execution, tests, network, or model calls.

overall.grade_scope: an arithmetic roll-up of model-assigned pass/fail from a source read, not a test result, scan, or certification
